segments.s074-g1: چنین introduces reported speech ('thus'/'so') and is followed by the verb گفت (here 'said'); in classical Persian the verb may precede the subject. پس is a discourse adverb 'then/thereupon'. شاه گردن‌فراز is the subject with گردن‌فراز an epithet attached post‑nominally (literally 'neck‑raising' = 'proud').
segments.s074-g2: کزین = 'from this/about this' introduces the topic. هرچه (a free relative) = 'whatever' and is modified by the verb گفتید ('you said', 2nd‑person plural past) which forms the relative clause. دارید راز = 'you have/hold a secret' (دارید present/past‑sense 'possess' here). The whole unit reports the content of the king's speech.
segments.s074-g3: بخواند is an archaic/past narrative form meaning 'he summoned/called' (verb before subject is common in poetry). آن زمان = 'at that time' (آن here = 'that/then'). زال را is the (definite) object marked by را and شهریار is the subject ('the king') placed after the object.
segments.s074-g4: کزو = 'from him/whom' (refers back to Zāl). خواست کردن is a periphrastic construction: خواست (wanted) + کردن (light verb) = 'wished to/was going to'. سخن خواستار functions as the verbal object/purpose: 'to obtain speech' or 'to question him' (خواستار here marks the desire/request). Overall it gives the king's purpose in summoning Zāl.
segments.s074-g5: بدان is a compact/archaised purpose cue here ('to that') formed from a prefix + دان; تا introduces purpose ('so that/in order that') and governs the subjunctive بپرسند ('they may ask'). ازو = 'from him', and چند چیز = 'several things'/'some matters' (چند + چیز).
segments.s074-g6: نهفته (hidden) qualifies سخنهای (the pluralized form of سخن, 'words/tales') and دیرینه ('ancient/long‑standing') further specifies their age; نیز = 'also' ties these items to what was to be asked. Adjectives and modifiers may appear before or after the noun in poetic Persian; here they form a tight modifying cluster.
segments.s074-g7: نشستند is the 3rd‑person plural past 'they sat down' (the provided lemma list is incorrect); بیدار دل is a compound attributive phrase 'awake‑hearted' or 'with alert minds/hearts' modifying the group; بخردان = 'the wise ones' (plural), so the phrase means 'the wise, with attentive hearts, sat down.'
segments.s074-g8: همان = 'that/same' identifies Zāl explicitly; زال is the hero's name. با marks accompaniment 'with', and نامور موبدان = 'renowned mobeds/priests' (نامور modifying موبدان). The whole segment lists who accompanied the wise listeners.
segments.s074-g25: چنین گفت is a short reporting phrase meaning 'thus said' or 'said thus' (چنین = 'thus', گفت = 'said'). کان here introduces the following noun (مرغزار) in a somewhat archaic/simile sense ('as/that meadow' or 'that very meadow'); مرغزار is the noun 'meadow' and stands as the topic of the reported utterance.
segments.s074-g26: This is a relative clause introduced by که ('that/which'). بینی is second person singular present of دیدن in an older form meaning 'you see'; it functions as the verb of perception. پر از is a postpositional construction meaning 'full of' (پر + از), followed by the coordinated nouns سبزه و جویبار 'green growth and stream(s)'. Order is subject (implicit: meadow) → relative clause verb (بینی) → predicate 'full of X'.
segments.s074-g27: یکی مرد is a common indefinite phrase 'a certain man' (یکی = 'a certain/someone', مرد = 'man'). با + noun ('با تیز داسی') expresses the instrument or accompaniment 'with a sharp sickle'; adjectives follow the noun in this line ('تیز' = 'sharp', 'داسی' = 'sickle') and بزرگ qualifies the whole (large).
segments.s074-g28: سوی ... اندر آید is a motion clause: سوی مرُغزار 'toward the meadow' + اندر آید 'enters / comes in'. اندر is a preposition/adverb 'into/in', and آید is third person present/past-form used here as 'came/comes'. سترگ follows as a short descriptive tag meaning 'in sight' / 'stately' (poetic), modifying the manner of appearance — here best read as 'he came into view' or 'he appeared, notable in sight.'
segments.s074-g29: همی is an adverb meaning 'continuously / kept on'; بدرود here is an older/poetic verb meaning 'reaps / cuts' (context: mowing). آن گیا 'that plant' is the direct object; خشک و‌تر is a coordination 'dry and wet/green' indicating he cuts both kinds. Word order: adverb + verb + object + coordinated adjectives.
segments.s074-g30: This clause is negative: نه بردارد 'does not take/does not remove' (نه negates the verb), او 'he' is the subject, هیچ intensifies as 'any/none', and ازآن کار سر is a prepositional/descriptive phrase 'of that work's head(s)' or 'any head from that [plant/work]'. Grammatically بردارد is a present/imperative-form verb in classical diction used here as simple negative present: 'he does not take away any head of it.'
segments.s074-g31: دگر گفت = 'another said' (دگر = 'another/other', گفت = 'said'). کان introduces a comparison/simile 'as if', and برکشیده is a past participle 'having raised' or 'raised up' used predicatively; دو سرو (two cypresses) is its object. The construction is an eyewitness-simile: 'another (observer) said, as if (he) had raised two cypresses.'
segments.s074-g32: ز دریای با موج is a circumstantial phrase 'from the sea with a wave' (ز = 'from', دریای = genitive/poetic form of 'sea', با موج = 'with a wave'). برسان is a present/relative verb 'that brings / bringing' (linking back to 'two cypresses' as if the sea brings them), and غرو 'sunset' gives the temporal image ('at sunset'). Together they form the poetic image 'brought from the sea by a wave at sunset.'
segments.s074-g49: بدان here is the classical contraction بدَ + آن (= بهِ آن) meaning 'to that' or 'upon that'; شارستان‌شان is 'their city' with the possessive suffix -شان attached to شارستان; نیاز آورد is a verbal phrase in the simple past (آورد) literally 'brought (a) need' — idiomatically 'need came/appeared'. The verb governs the whole clause and ties back to the locative phrase بدان.
segments.s074-g50: هم is an adverb here ('also' / 'likewise'); اندیشگان is a plural agent noun 'the thoughtful, the wise'; دراز follows the noun as an attributive adjective ('long' or 'long‑thinking/far‑thinking' in a poetic sense); آوُرد is again the past verbal form 'brought/came' and completes the clause — the phrase means roughly 'the long‑thinking (wise) ones too came/appeared.'
segments.s074-g51: به پرده درست is a prepositional phrase ('to/at the proper curtain' — i.e. 'in proper privacy'); این سخنها is the direct object 'these words' (سخنها = plural); بجوی is the present/jussive form of جویدن/جست‌و جو (here 'seek') used as an imperative/request: 'seek' or 'look for'. Word order places the locative/adverbial phrase before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s074-g52: به پیش places the action 'in the presence of' or 'before'; ردان (an older/formal plural participial noun) denotes 'those who reject/drive away' in this line; آشکارا is an adverb 'openly'; بگوی is the present/jussive form of گفتن used as an imperative 'say' or 'state'. Together: 'say openly before the rejecters.'
segments.s074-g53: گر is the conditional 'if'; این رازها is the subject/object 'these secrets'; آشکارا is an adverb 'openly, plainly'; کنی is the present subjunctive / jussive of کردن used for the protasis of the condition ('if you reveal / if you do ...'). The clause thus reads 'If you reveal these secrets openly...'.
segments.s074-g54: ز is the classical preposition 'from' (poetic form of از); خاک سیه is 'black earth/dust' (سیه = black, adjective after noun is common in older Persian); مشک سارا is a possessive construction without an overt ezāfe ('the musk of Sārā' — classical poetry often omits the ezāfe); کنی is again the subjunctive/present form of کردن completing the apodosis ('you will make' / 'you will render'). The line warns of a negative result: 'you will make Sara's musk into black earth.'
segments.s074-g41: خرامند is an adjectival/participle form placed before مردم and describes their manner — 'striding proudly' or 'swaggering'. مردم is the noun 'people' (the subject). ازان is a prepositional/relational particle 'from that' linking the people to the previously mentioned town (شارستان = 'city, town').
segments.s074-g42: گرفته is a past participle/verb meaning 'taken/seized' (here the action of the people); the subject ('the people') is understood from the previous segment. به هامون marks direction 'into/to the plain' and یکی خارستان is the object phrase 'a certain thorn‑thicket' (یکی = indefinite 'a certain', خارستان = 'thorny thicket/wasteland').
segments.s074-g43: بناها is the plural noun 'buildings/structures' (subject). کشیدند is the past verb 'they drew/raised/erected' used here with بناها as its object; سر means 'top, summit' (the thing raised). تا ... به ماه is a comparative/limit phrase 'up to the moon' (تا = 'until/up to', به ماه = 'to the moon'), a hyperbolic way of saying the tops were very high.
segments.s074-g44: پرستنده is an agent-noun 'worshipper/devotee' used as a predicate noun. گشتند is the past/plural verb 'they became' (here linking to پرستنده). و هم ('and also') links a second predicate, and پیشگاه ('the presence/court') is another nominal complement — so the clause says they became worshippers and likewise (were) in the court's presence; the verb گشتند governs both complements.
segments.s074-g45: وزآن is a conjunctional compound 'and from that' linking back to what was said. شارستان‌شان ('their town') is the referent (شارستان + possessive شان). به دل نگذرد is an idiomatic verbal phrase: نگذرد (negative of گذشتن) 'does not pass' with به دل 'from the heart' — i.e. 'does not leave the heart' or 'is not forgotten'.
segments.s074-g46: کس here functions with the negative predicate to mean 'no one' (the universal negation is expressed by the negative verb that follows). از + یاد کردن is the light‑verb construction 'to mention/remember' (از marks the source/act of remembering), سخن is the object 'speech/word', and نشمرد is the negative past form of 'to count/consider' used idiomatically as 'did not omit / did not fail to [speak of]' — i.e. 'no one failed to speak of it.'
segments.s074-g47: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain / someone'. بومهین (بوم + ِـهین) means 'the homeland/that region'. خیزد is the verb 'rises/starts up' (third person singular); in epic narrative this present form is often used as a vivid/historical present to report a sudden action. از ناگهان is an adverbial phrase 'suddenly'.
segments.s074-g48: بر و بومشان is a compound-like phrase meaning 'their land and dwelling' (بر = 'upon/over' used here as part of the collocation for territory; بومشان = 'their homeland'). پاک is an intensifier 'completely/utterly'. گردد is the present/subjunctive form of 'become' (here as narrative 'became/was made'), and نهان is the predicate adjective 'hidden/vanished' — together: 'their realm became completely hidden/vanished.'
segments.s074-g17: یکی زان به کردار دریای قار = 'one of them in the manner of the Sea of Qar'. یکی (one) introduces a single member of the group; زان is an archaic/literary form meaning 'of those' or 'from that'. به کردار is a prepositional phrase 'in the manner/behavior of' (به + abstract noun), and دریای قار is a genitive construction (دریا 'sea' + قار as a following modifier/name). 'قار' here is a poetic name/quality attached to دریا.
segments.s074-g18: یکی چون بلور سپید آبدار = 'another like crystal, white and moist/lustrous.' یکی again marks another one; چون is the simile 'like/as'. The noun phrase بلور سپید آبدار stacks modifiers after the head (بلور 'crystal' modified by سپید 'white' and آبدار 'moist/lustrous'), a normal poetic descriptive order.
segments.s074-g19: بجنبید و هر دو شتابنده‌اند = 'they moved/stirred, and both are hurrying/quick.' جنبید here is a narrative/finite verb meaning 'moved' or 'stirred' (classical/poetic form), joined by و 'and' to the predicate هر دو شتابنده‌اند, where هر دو 'both' + شتابنده‌اند 'they are hurrying/are swift' describes their motion.
segments.s074-g20: همان یکدیگر را نیابنده‌اند = 'yet/indeed they do not find one another.' همان here is an emphatic/confirmatory 'just/indeed'; یکدیگر را is the definite object 'one another' (را marks the object); نیابنده‌اند is a compound with negative prefix ن‑ + یابنده (present participle 'findable/finding') plus اند, giving the sense 'they are not to be found / they cannot be reached' (i.e., they fail to find one another).
segments.s074-g21: سه‌دیگر چنین گفت کان سی سوار = 'the three together said thus: that thirty riders…' سه‌دیگر is a collective expression 'the three together'; چنین گفت is the fixed phrase 'said thus' (چنین + گفت 'said'); کان introduces the reported clause (classical usage of کان ~ 'that/as follows').
segments.s074-g22: کجا بگذرانند بر شهریار = 'where will (they) pass/spend (time) before the king?' کجا is 'where'; بگذرانند is a present/future subjunctive/plural form meaning 'they will/pass (time), spend' (here 'pass/ride by' or 'perform before'); بر + شهریار is 'before/to the king' (بر used as 'toward/upon' or 'in the presence of' the monarch). The clause is interrogative/indirect, introduced by کان above.
segments.s074-g23: یکی کم شود باز چون بشمری = 'one becomes fewer/missing when you count (again).' یکی 'one (of them)'; کم شود is a present/passive‑looking form 'becomes few/less' (شدن + کم); باز 'again/still' links to the next clause; چون بشمری uses چون in a temporal/conditional sense 'when/if' (poetic usage) + بشمری 'you count/reckon' (second‑person present/subjunctive form).
segments.s074-g24: همان سی بود باز چون بنگری = 'he was still the same thirty when you look again.' همان سی بود 'he/they were the same thirty' (همان = 'the same/that very'); بود is the past copula 'was'; باز 'again/yet' + چون بنگری ('when you look' — چون used temporally and بنگری is the present/subjunctive 'you look/regard'). Together this contrasts counting with appearance.
segments.s074-g33: یکی مرغ دارد بریشان کنام = 'A certain bird has (a) resting-place among them.' یکی here means 'a certain' (indefinite one). دارد is the present-tense possessive 'has'. بریشان is the fused prepositional phrase بر + ایشان (on/among them) and کنام is an archaic noun 'resting-place / bosom / nest' that functions as the possessed noun.
segments.s074-g34: نشیمش به شام آن بود این به بام = 'At evening it would sit on that one; this (it) on the rooftop.' نشیمش is an old/narrative verbal form (cf. نشیند) with an enclitic -ش = 'it' (third person), used here like 'it sits/would sit.' به شام = 'at evening' (به + شام). آن بود = 'that was' (literal 'that was/was that') and این به بام = 'this [was] on the roof'; the copula is omitted, common in Persian.
segments.s074-g35: ازین چون بپرد شود برگ خشک = 'When/from this (از این) a dry leaf flies (is made to fly).' ازین is از + این ('from this'); چون here is the subordinating conjunction 'when/as.' بپرد is the verbal root 'fly/skip' and شود is the auxiliary 'become / be made' used here to form the event ('is made to fly' / 'flies off'). برگ خشک is the noun phrase 'dry leaf' that is the item set in motion.
segments.s074-g36: برآن بر نشیند دهد بوی مشک = 'It alights upon that (one) and emits the scent of musk.' برآن functions as 'on/onto that' (بر + آن); the repeated بر + نشیند ('alight / sit down') emphasizes the physical alighting. دهد is the present/habitual verb 'gives/emits' and بوی مشک is the direct object 'the scent of musk.' The clause mixes narrative verbal forms common in epic diction.
segments.s074-g37: ازآن دو همیشه یکی آبدار = 'Of those two, one is always juicy/lush.' ازآن دو is a genitive-like phrase 'of those two'; همیشه is the adverb 'always'; یکی آبدار is a nominal predicate 'one (is) juicy' with the copula omitted (typical in Persian).
segments.s074-g38: یکی پژمریده شده سوگوار = 'One (the other) has become withered and sorrowful.' یکی = 'one (the other)'; پژمریده is a past-participial/adjectival 'withered'; شده is the auxiliary/verb 'has become' linking the participle to the following predicative سوگوار 'mourning/sorrowful.' The sequence expresses resultant state.
segments.s074-g39: بپرسید دیگر که بر کوهسار = 'Ask further / ask another (question) that on the mountainside ...' بپرسید is a past/narrative form 'asked' or an imperative frame depending on context; here it introduces a request for further asking or a narrative 'they asked more.' دیگر means 'another / further,' که introduces the subordinate clause, and بر کوهسار = 'on the mountainside.'
segments.s074-g40: یکی شارستان یافتم استوار = 'I found a certain city, firm/steadfast.' یکی = 'one / a certain'; شارستان is 'city/town' (the direct object of یافتم); یافتم is first-person past 'I found'; استوار is an adjective 'steady/firm' qualifying the city. Persian often places the adjective after the noun it modifies.
segments.s074-g9: بپرسید is a past-tense narrative verb here ('asked'); the phrase shows literary V–O–S order: the verb comes first, then objects. مر is an old dative/pronominal form ('to/for me' or ‘of me’) placed before the object; زال را is the definite direct object (زال marked by را); موبدی at the end names the agent (the mobad) — a common poetic inversion where the subject follows the object and verb.
segments.s074-g10: ازین = از + این ('from this') with the enclitic/orthographic ین; تیزهش is a possessive compound ('its tip/point') where -ش = 'its'. راه‌بین is a compound noun 'path‑seer' or 'one who sees the way'; بخردی is an adjectival/genitive phrase 'of wisdom' (literally 'with wisdom' or 'wise'), modifying راه‌بین. Together this segment is an object/phrase that the verb in the previous segment introduces.
segments.s074-g11: که introduces a relative clause ('which' / 'that'). از ... ده و دو تای is a numeric expression: از + 'ده و دو' (ten and two = twelve) with تای functioning as a counting/unit marker; سرو سهی is a compound noun phrase 'threefold/poetic cypress' (سهی here is a poetic epithet attached to سرو). The whole phrase identifies quantity/type.
segments.s074-g12: که again begins a subordinate clause ('which'). رسته‌ست is a perfect/resultative construction ('has grown' or 'is grown') formed from the past participle رسته plus the copula ست (is/has). شاداب is an adjective 'fresh/vigorous'; با + فرهی is a prepositional phrase 'with nobility' where فرهی is an adjective/noun 'noble, cultured' used attributively. Together: 'which has grown up fresh with nobility.'
segments.s074-g13: ازآن is an adverbial phrase 'from that' or 'of that'. برزده is a participle/adjective 'lofty, uplifted' describing the trees. هر یکی = 'each one' (هر + یکی). شاخ سی is the noun phrase 'thirty branches' (شاخ = branch/bough and سی = thirty; the numeral appears in the line though is not listed as a separate token in the word list). Grammatically this gives the quantity produced by each tall tree.
segments.s074-g14: نگردد is a negative/resultative verb form 'it does not become/it is not turned' (third person); here it functions to state invariability. کم و بیش = 'less and more' (i.e., fewer or more); در پارسی = 'in Persian' (language/idiom). The clause asserts that in Persian usage the number is not fewer or greater — it is exact.
segments.s074-g15: دگر introduces a different/another speaker ('another'). موبدی is the agent 'the mobad' (Zoroastrian priest). گفت is the past tense verb 'said'. کای is a vocative interjection 'O' used to address someone directly; سرفراز is the vocative noun 'exalted/proud one' being addressed. The segment reports a new speaker addressing someone honorifically.
segments.s074-g16: دو اسپ is a simple numeral + noun 'two horses/steeds' (دو = two, اسپ = steed). گرانمایه and تیزتاز are postpositive adjectives: گرانمایه 'precious/esteemed' and تیزتاز 'swift/brisk'; و simply coordinates them. The phrase is an appositive direct address or the beginning of the description of the horses being mentioned.

sentences.s074-s1: Thus spoke the proud‑necked king: whatever you have said about this you keep secret.
sentences.s074-s2: At that time the king summoned Zāl, for from him he wished to obtain speech (i.e., he wished to question him).
sentences.s074-s3: To that end, so that they might ask him several matters — also hidden, long‑ancient tales.
sentences.s074-s4: The wise, with awakened hearts, sat down — Zāl himself together with the famed mobeds.
sentences.s074-s13: The fourth said thus: 'That meadow which you see is full of green growth and streams.'
sentences.s074-s14: A man with a large sharp sickle came toward the meadow and came into view.
sentences.s074-s15: He kept cutting that plant, both dry and green; he did not spare any of its heads in that work.
sentences.s074-s16: Another said: 'It was as if he had raised two cypresses, brought from the sea by a wave at sunset.'
sentences.s074-s25: To that city their need came; likewise the thoughtful ones came (or were brought).
sentences.s074-s26: Seek these words rightly behind the curtain; state them openly before those who reject (you).
sentences.s074-s27: If you make these secrets public, you will turn Sara's musk into black earth.
sentences.s074-s21: The people, striding proudly from that town, took to the plain a certain thorny waste.
sentences.s074-s22: They raised buildings whose tops reached up to the moon; they became worshippers and were also in the royal presence.
sentences.s074-s23: And from that their city never leaves the heart; no one fails to speak of it.
sentences.s074-s24: Suddenly one from that homeland rose up, and their realm became wholly hidden.
sentences.s074-s9: One of them, in bearing like the Sea of Qar; another like crystal, white and dewy.
sentences.s074-s10: They moved, and both were speeding; nevertheless they could not find one another.
sentences.s074-s11: The three together said thus: 'Where will thirty horsemen pass before the king?'
sentences.s074-s12: One may be missing when you count, yet when you look again he is still the same thirty.
sentences.s074-s17: A certain bird has its resting-place among them; at evening it sits on that one, and at (the) rooftop this one.
sentences.s074-s18: When from this a dry leaf is made to fly away, it alights upon that one and gives off the scent of musk.
sentences.s074-s19: Of those two, one is always lush and juicy, the other has withered and become sorrowful.
sentences.s074-s20: Ask further: on the mountainside I found a certain city, firm and steadfast.
sentences.s074-s5: The mobad asked Zāl about it, saying: “From this very sharp tip, [is there] a wise path‑seer?”
sentences.s074-s6: — which is one of the twelve (ten-and-two) threefold cypresses, that has grown up fresh and noble.
sentences.s074-s7: From each of those lofty ones thirty branches grow; in Persian it is neither less nor more.
sentences.s074-s8: Another mobad said: 'O exalted one, two precious and swift steeds ...'
